posture: Watch (ran clean) — no fresh acute trigger surfaced across the free sources in the last 24 hours, extending the quiet end-of-week cycle. No new political, delivery, supply-chain, market, safety or cyber signal met the relevance bar today, so no items are logged. The standing exposures are unchanged and carried forward: the New England REZ Access Scheme draft declaration remains unconfirmed as released (now several days past its end-June target), EnergyCo's New England corridor field-investigation campaign is running through the corridor July–September, and the DPHI investigation into ACEREZ's destruction of an Aboriginal rock shelter on the Central-West Orana build is still open.
summary: A full scan of the free source register returned no new NSW-REZ-relevant development in the last 24 hours; every thread that came up (New England access scheme, ACEREZ heritage investigation, the final 2026 ISP, the Coalition's pledge to cap New England and scrap the Bayswater link, the July–September field-investigation program) is a prior-digest item with no fresh movement and is deduplicated out per the writing rules. Today is therefore published with empty sections as a continuity / 'ran clean' marker. The most material standing signal remains procedural: EnergyCo's target of releasing the New England REZ Access Scheme draft declaration by end June has still not been confirmed as exhibited on the agency's access-scheme page, keeping a low-grade schedule-slippage watch live ahead of the end-September ministerial declaration.
